figures.caption: Figure 12 The gray region is Q(ν) for the right child ν of ν̂. The two non-base sides of △ν are marked with green dashed segments. The orange dashed segment on the base side πν of △ν is the left bridge lν of πν . In this case, ν cannot have a left subtree in TQ.
Figure 13 Illustrating the geodesic triangles △ν̂ (the gray region) and △ν (the green region). For any µ in the right subtree of ν, △µ must be “right” of △ν .
Figure 6 The visibility of q in P \ Pπ is constrained by the cone Cq(s).

Figure 10 Illustrating the case a ≠ pj . The green region is Q ab (△k). The union of the green region and the gray region is Q. The red solid polygonal chain is in B1 and the blue solid polygonal chain is in B2.
Table 1 Summary of the results for visibility queries (k is the output size).
Figure 7 The shaded region bounded by d is a pocket Pd(△∗). a∗b∗ is the bride d∗ on ∂△∗ \ dv∗ . The bigger shaded region bounded by d∗ is Pd∗ (△∗).
Figure 8 The gray region Q is an open B1-bounded subpocket while the green region Q′ is a closed B2-bounded subpocket.

Figure 11 The green region is Q ab (△ν). The union of the green region and the gray region is Pd(△ν). The red solid polygonal chain is in B1 and the blue polygonal chain is in B2. The portion of B1 from pi to b is B1(Q ab (△ν)).
